title: `image is in use` on `podman system reset`
body:
### Issue Description

`podman 4.8.0` with sqlite database backend (affected by #20809) failed on `system reset` with:
```
ERRO[0014] image used by 929b991f97c04ce7d5a203abb206626c2285e63fe45e9b690a44fce236006417: image is in use by a container: consider listing external containers and force-removing image 
```

Three different containers based on three different images were running at the time.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Note that issue seems to be racey and there are no steps to reproduce that would always work. In my case it was:
1. Create 3 (running) containers
2. Perform `podman system reset`

### Describe the results you received

Error occurred.

### Describe the results you expected

No Error.

### podman info output

comments.body: If you try to reproduce please add `--log-level=debug` to the command, that should give some insight.
also, if you can reproduce, more information about the exact reproducer would be great ... like ... are all the running continers using the same image or ?

Doing my best with reproduction attempt but this one is tough. Although I did get a database locked that was logged only with debug level:

didn't result in `image is in use` though.

> are all the running continers using the same image or ?

this one time that it happened each of 3 containers was based on independent image without any common layers.

Got another error instance with debug logging enabled. Same situation as before -- 3 running containers based on 3 independent images (full log: [system-reset-debug.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/13519334/system-reset-debug.txt)):

```console
ERRO[0013] image used by 111c8776b2ad752b724a7fc3a559b14ee5df9702f23b0577ec73db537cbb9562: image is in use by a container: consider listing external containers and force-removing image
```

With the culprit likely being:

```console
DEBU[0009] Failed to remove container 111c8776b2ad752b724a7fc3a559b14ee5df9702f23b0577ec73db537cbb9562: cannot remove container 111c8776b2ad752b724a7fc3a559b14ee5df9702f23b0577ec73db537cbb9562 as it could not be stopped: unmarshalling container 111c8776b2ad752b724a7fc3a559b14ee5df9702f23b0577ec73db537cbb9562 state JSON: readObjectStart: expect { or n, but found , error found in #0 byte of ...||..., bigger context ...||...
```

The error is interesting in two ways:
1. It's actually the only container out of 3 that didn't get `database locked` during setup.
2. Follow up `podman system reset` does not see any problem:

Similar issue just reoccurred but:
- x86_64
- kernel 6.6.6
- podman 4.8.1
- boltdb
- conmon 2.1.8

I know the command has invalid argument placement but it didn'tfail on wrong executable but instead with:
```
$ podman run --rm -i -t docker.io/alpine --arch i386 bash
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 661ff4d9561e done   | 
Copying config f8c20f8bbc done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
Error: container create failed (no logs from conmon): conmon bytes "": readObjectStart: expect { or n, but found , error found in #0 byte of ...||..., bigger context ...||...
```

maybe you faced an external containers?
`podman ps -a --external`
closing the issue since https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21226 was merged
